billing.pageSubtitle: Each optimization run costs 1 credit. Buy packs to keep running analyses.

billing.usdOnlyNote: We charge in USD. If your card is from another country, your bank handles the FX.
billing.cryptoNote: Pay with USDC or USDT. The amount is set in USD; the payment network handles the 1:1 settlement.

step1.title: Your location
step1.description: Confirm these to personalize the app. You can change them anytime.
step1.whyCountry: We use your country to suggest local assets and the right risk-free rate (CETES in Mexico, T-bills in the US, etc.).
step1.whyCurrency: Returns and amounts are displayed in this currency.

step2.title: Your investor profile
step2.description: This calibrates the app's language and the strategies we recommend.
step2.riskQuestion: If your portfolio dropped 30% in one month, what would you do?
step2.whyRisk: Your reaction to a sharp drawdown reveals your real risk tolerance better than asking you to classify yourself.

step3.title: Your markets of interest
step3.description: We tailor ticker suggestions and educational content to what you care about.
step3.marketsLabel: Markets of interest (select one or more)
step3.marketsHint: Pick at least one or write your own below.
step3.conceptsLabel: Which concepts are you familiar with? (optional)
step3.whyConcepts: If these are new to you, we'll show explanatory tooltips and highlight the Academy section.

page.footer: End of the journey · Figures shown are illustrative

welcome.title: First time investing?
welcome.description: Walk through the Top-Down guide in five stops. From the global map to your portfolio weights, in about five minutes.

zoomIntro.description: Investing like a professional doesn't start with hunting for tickers. It starts by looking at the world. We'll zoom in: from the planet to your portfolio, in five stops.

macro.label: The world
macro.title: What economic climate are we in?
macro.tagline: Before picking a stock, pick the playing field.
macro.summary: The first zoom is the broadest. You look at the global economy and ask whether the wind is at your back.
macro.bullet1: Pick the region where you want to operate (US, Europe, Emerging Markets).
macro.bullet2: Watch the cycle: interest rates, inflation, growth.
macro.bullet3: Check market sentiment: euphoria and panic often signal turning points.

allocation.label: Your profile
allocation.title: How much risk can you take?
allocation.tagline: Before the ticker, the mix between growth and safety.
allocation.summary: Define your asset allocation: the percentage of your capital in equities (growth) vs. fixed income (stability).
allocation.bullet2: Moderate (60/40): the classic balance between growth and protection.
allocation.bullet3: Aggressive (90/10): maximum long-term return while accepting large drawdowns.

sectors.label: The sectors
sectors.title: Follow the institutional money
sectors.tagline: Not every sector wins at the same time.
sectors.summary: When rates are low, technology shines. With high inflation, energy and staples lead. Capital rotates with the cycle.
sectors.bullet1: Cyclical sectors: technology, consumer discretionary, industrials.
sectors.bullet2: Defensive sectors: consumer staples, healthcare, utilities.
sectors.bullet3: Sector rotation anticipates phase changes in the business cycle.

assets.label: The assets
assets.title: Quality and timing: the dual filter
assets.tagline: A good company at a bad price is still a bad investment.
assets.summary: Once the sector is chosen, you cross fundamental analysis (what to buy?) with technical analysis (when to enter?).
assets.bullet1: Fundamentals: solid balance sheet, competitive advantage (moat), ethical governance.
assets.bullet2: Valuation: current price vs. intrinsic value and historical average.
assets.bullet3: Technicals: support, resistance, momentum (RSI, MACD, moving averages).

portfolio.label: The portfolio
portfolio.title: Optimization and risk management
portfolio.tagline: Here's where Markowitz comes in: exact weights on the efficient frontier.
portfolio.summary: Combine lowly correlated assets to maximize the risk-adjusted return (Sharpe). And always have an exit plan.
portfolio.bullet1: Low correlations are what make diversification truly work.
portfolio.bullet2: The efficient frontier shows the best portfolio for each level of risk.
portfolio.bullet3: Define your stop-loss before entering: capital preservation is the priority.

station1.expansionDescription: Low rates, healthy growth, strong employment. Optimism dominates the mood.
station1.contractionDescription: Activity falling, layoffs, more expensive credit. Capital seeks shelter in defensive assets.
station1.stagflationDescription: High inflation while the economy stalls. The worst combination: savings losing real value.

station2.drawdownCaption: Illustrative curve. Represents a hypothetical path with a recession halfway through.
station2.conservativeDescription: Capital preservation first. Low volatility, modest returns, few large drawdowns.
station2.moderateDescription: The classic balance. Participates in growth but cushions the blows with fixed income.
station2.aggressiveDescription: Maximum long-term growth. Expect large drawdowns during recessions; you have to hold on.

station3.earlyDescription: Low rates, confidence returning. Capital rotates toward cyclicals.
station3.midDescription: Sustained growth. Leadership broadens to industrials.
station3.lateDescription: Inflation rises, rates rise. Commodities and defensives shine.
station3.recessionDescription: The market takes refuge in the most stable names.

station4.alphaName: Company with a strong competitive advantage
station4.betaName: Sector leader trading at a hot price
station4.deltaName: Good price but a weak business

station5.correlationDescription: Red = move together (poor diversification). Blue = move opposite (good diversification).
station5.correlationHoverHint: Hover over a cell to interpret it.
station5.correlationNegative: negative correlation: they complement each other well.
station5.correlationLow: low correlation: good candidates to combine.
station5.frontierDescription: Each point is a portfolio. The curve marks the optimums: maximum return for each level of risk.
station5.frontierCaption: Illustration. In the real app you use your own tickers and dates.
station5.ctaDescription: Build your ticker list, set the date range, and let the optimizer find the optimal weights on the efficient frontier.
